message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# GitHub CI: Test + Deploy to GitHub Pages

## Context

The game is a SvelteKit static site (adapter-static). We want pushes to `main` to:
1. Run the full test suite (Vitest with Playwright/Chromium for browser tests, Node for server tests)
2. Build and publish to GitHub Pages on success

No CI exists yet. The only complication is that GitHub Pages project deployments sit at a subpath (`username.github.io/claudia`), so SvelteKit's `paths.base` must be configured at build time.

---

## Files to create / modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | Create (new) |
| `svelte.config.js` | Minimal edit — add `paths.base` |

---

## 1. `svelte.config.js` change

Add `paths: { base: process.env.BASE_PATH ?? '' }` to the `kit` config. `BASE_PATH` is injected by the CI workflow; locally it is absent so `base` defaults to `''` — no change to dev behaviour.

```js
import { mdsvex } from 'mdsvex';
import adapter from '@sveltejs/adapter-static';

/** @type {import('@sveltejs/kit').Config} */
const config = {
  kit: {
    adapter: adapter(),
    // BASE_PATH injected by CI (actions/configure-pages). Empty locally.
    paths: { base: process.env.BASE_PATH ?? '' }
  },
  preprocess: [mdsvex()],
  extensions: ['.svelte', '.svx']
};

export default config;
```

---

## 2. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

Three-job pipeline: `test` → `build` → `deploy`.

```yaml
name: Deploy to GitHub Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

# Don't cancel a deployment in flight; queue the next one.
concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Playwright image ships Chromium + system deps pre-installed,
    # matching the playwright ^1.58.2 devDependency.
    container:
      image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.58.2-noble
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - uses: actions/configure-pages@v4
        id: pages
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
        env:
          BASE_PATH: ${{ steps.pages.outputs.base_path }}
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: build/

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

---

## One-time GitHub repo settings

In **Settings → Pages → Build and deployment source**, select **GitHub Actions** (not "Deploy from a branch"). This enables the OIDC-based deploy that `actions/deploy-pages@v4` requires.

---

## Verification

1. Confirm `npm run build` still works locally after the `svelte.config.js` change (BASE_PATH absent → base `''`).
2. Push to `main` and watch the Actions tab — jobs should run sequentially: test → build → deploy.
3. After first successful deploy, visit the Pages URL and verify the site loads correctly.
4. Audit any hardcoded `href="/"` in components — on a subpath they need `import { base } from '$app/paths'` prefixing.


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/skye/.claude/projects/-Users-skye-workbench-claudia/cb620dac-92ee-42a3-9ed6-f908832f61bc.jsonl

planContent:
# GitHub CI: Test + Deploy to GitHub Pages

## Context

The game is a SvelteKit static site (adapter-static). We want pushes to `main` to:
1. Run the full test suite (Vitest with Playwright/Chromium for browser tests, Node for server tests)
2. Build and publish to GitHub Pages on success

No CI exists yet. The only complication is that GitHub Pages project deployments sit at a subpath (`username.github.io/claudia`), so SvelteKit's `paths.base` must be configured at build time.

---

## Files to create / modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` | Create (new) |
| `svelte.config.js` | Minimal edit — add `paths.base` |

---

## 1. `svelte.config.js` change

Add `paths: { base: process.env.BASE_PATH ?? '' }` to the `kit` config. `BASE_PATH` is injected by the CI workflow; locally it is absent so `base` defaults to `''` — no change to dev behaviour.

```js
import { mdsvex } from 'mdsvex';
import adapter from '@sveltejs/adapter-static';

/** @type {import('@sveltejs/kit').Config} */
const config = {
  kit: {
    adapter: adapter(),
    // BASE_PATH injected by CI (actions/configure-pages). Empty locally.
    paths: { base: process.env.BASE_PATH ?? '' }
  },
  preprocess: [mdsvex()],
  extensions: ['.svelte', '.svx']
};

export default config;
```

---

## 2. `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

Three-job pipeline: `test` → `build` → `deploy`.

```yaml
name: Deploy to GitHub Pages

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

# Don't cancel a deployment in flight; queue the next one.
concurrency:
  group: pages
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Playwright image ships Chromium + system deps pre-installed,
    # matching the playwright ^1.58.2 devDependency.
    container:
      image: mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.58.2-noble
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: npm
      - uses: actions/configure-pages@v4
        id: pages
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run build
        env:
          BASE_PATH: ${{ steps.pages.outputs.base_path }}
      - uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: build/

  deploy:
    needs: build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment:
      name: github-pages
      url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }}
    steps:
      - id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

---

## One-time GitHub repo settings

In **Settings → Pages → Build and deployment source**, select **GitHub Actions** (not "Deploy from a branch"). This enables the OIDC-based deploy that `actions/deploy-pages@v4` requires.

---

## Verification

1. Confirm `npm run build` still works locally after the `svelte.config.js` change (BASE_PATH absent → base `''`).
2. Push to `main` and watch the Actions tab — jobs should run sequentially: test → build → deploy.
3. After first successful deploy, visit the Pages URL and verify the site loads correctly.
4. Audit any hardcoded `href="/"` in components — on a subpath they need `import { base } from '$app/paths'` prefixing.

content.thinking: Let me start by reading the project context documents and the current svelte.config.js to understand the existing setup before making changes.

content.content:
{"result":"# Claudia Project Overview

**Purpose:** Browser-based interaction fiction / chat app game (SvelteKit + Svelte 5)

**Tech stack:** SvelteKit, Svelte 5 (runes), TypeScript, Vitest + vitest-browser-svelte (Playwright/Chromium), fast-check (v4) for test arbitraries

**Key commands:**

- `npm run test` — run all tests (non-interactive)
- `npm run test:unit` — run tests in watch mode
- `npm run check` — svelte-check + TypeScript
- `npm run lint` — prettier + eslint
- `npm run format` — prettier write

**Test layout:** All tests live in `src/test/`, mirroring the source tree:

- `src/test/utils/arb.ts` — fast-check arbitraries for game types
- `src/test/lib/` — component tests
- `src/test/routes/` — page tests

**Important paths:**

- `src/lib/types.ts` — core types (ChatUser, ChatComponentState, ChatAppState, Markdown/md)
- `src/lib/state.svelte.ts` — shared reactive singleton (appState)
- `src/lib/chat/`, `src/lib/selector/`, `src/lib/input/` — component folders
- `src/routes/+page.svelte` — main page

**Test approach:** Use `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]` (single draw) not `fc.assert`; browser rendering too slow for multi-run. fc.alpha() removed in fc v4 — use `fc.stringMatching(/^[a-z]{3,16}$/)` instead.
"}

# Claudia Project Overview

**Purpose:** Browser-based interaction fiction / chat app game (SvelteKit + Svelte 5)

**Tech stack:** SvelteKit, Svelte 5 (runes), TypeScript, Vitest + vitest-browser-svelte (Playwright/Chromium), fast-check (v4) for test arbitraries

**Key commands:**

- `npm run test` — run all tests (non-interactive)
- `npm run test:unit` — run tests in watch mode
- `npm run check` — svelte-check + TypeScript
- `npm run lint` — prettier + eslint
- `npm run format` — prettier write

**Test layout:** All tests live in `src/test/`, mirroring the source tree:

- `src/test/utils/arb.ts` — fast-check arbitraries for game types
- `src/test/lib/` — component tests
- `src/test/routes/` — page tests

**Important paths:**

- `src/lib/types.ts` — core types (ChatUser, ChatComponentState, ChatAppState, Markdown/md)
- `src/lib/state.svelte.ts` — shared reactive singleton (appState)
- `src/lib/chat/`, `src/lib/selector/`, `src/lib/input/` — component folders
- `src/routes/+page.svelte` — main page

**Test approach:** Use `fc.sample(arb, 1)[0]` (single draw) not `fc.assert`; browser rendering too slow for multi-run. fc.alpha() removed in fc v4 — use `fc.stringMatching(/^[a-z]{3,16}$/)` instead.

input.command:
git add svelte.config.js .github/workflows/deploy.yml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add GitHub Actions CI/CD: test + deploy to GitHub Pages

- Three-job pipeline: test → build → deploy
- Test job runs in Playwright container (matches devDep playwright ^1.58.2)
- Build job uses actions/configure-pages to inject BASE_PATH for subpath routing
- Deploy job publishes static build artifact via OIDC

svelte.config.js: add paths.base driven by BASE_PATH env var (empty locally).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Done. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**`svelte.config.js`** — added `paths: { base: process.env.BASE_PATH ?? '' }` to the `kit` config. Locally `BASE_PATH` is absent so `base` stays `''`; in CI it gets the subpath from `actions/configure-pages`.

**`.github/workflows/deploy.yml`** — three-job pipeline:
- `test`: runs `npm test` inside the `mcr.microsoft.com/playwright:v1.58.2-noble` container (matches the devDependency, Chromium pre-installed)
- `build`: gates on `test`, uses `actions/configure-pages` to set `BASE_PATH`, builds and uploads the artifact
- `deploy`: gates on `build`, deploys via OIDC with `actions/deploy-pages@v4`

**Next step (one-time, in your browser):** Go to **Settings → Pages → Build and deployment source** and switch to **GitHub Actions**. Without that, the `deploy-pages` action won't have the permissions it needs.

Also worth a quick audit: any hardcoded `href="/"` in components will need `import { base } from '$app/paths'` to work correctly under the `/claudia` subpath.

message.content: It looks like ci fails because of our npm ci hook expecting a .git

content.thinking:
The user is saying that CI is failing because of an npm ci hook that expects a `.git` directory. This is likely a `prepare` script or a `postinstall` hook in `package.json` that runs something like `husky install` or similar git-related setup.

Let me look at the package.json to understand what hooks are configured.

content.text: The `prepare` script runs `git config core.hooksPath hooks` unconditionally after the `|| echo ''` short-circuits the sync failure — so it always runs, even in CI where there's no `.git`. The fix is to guard the git config call so it only runs when `.git` exists:

input.old_string: "prepare": "svelte-kit sync || echo '' && git config core.hooksPath hooks",
input.new_string: "prepare": "svelte-kit sync || true && (test -d .git && git config core.hooksPath hooks || true)",

content.content: The file <PROJECT_ROOT>/package.json has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.oldString: "prepare": "svelte-kit sync || echo '' && git config core.hooksPath hooks",
toolUseResult.newString: "prepare": "svelte-kit sync || true && (test -d .git && git config core.hooksPath hooks || true)",
toolUseResult.originalFile:
{
	"name": "claudia",
	"private": true,
	"version": "0.0.1",
	"type": "module",
	"scripts": {
		"dev": "vite dev",
		"build": "vite build",
		"preview": "vite preview",
		"prepare": "svelte-kit sync || echo '' && git config core.hooksPath hooks",
		"check": "svelte-kit sync && svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json",
		"check:watch": "svelte-kit sync && svelte-check --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json --watch",
		"lint": "prettier --check . && eslint .",
		"format": "prettier --write .",
		"test:unit": "vitest",
		"test": "npm run test:unit -- --run"
	},
	"devDependencies": {
		"@djankies/vitest-mcp": "^0.5.1",
		"@eslint/compat": "^2.0.2",
		"@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
		"@sveltejs/adapter-static": "^3.0.10",
		"@sveltejs/kit": "^2.50.2",
		"@sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte": "^6.2.4",
		"@types/node": "^22",
		"@vitest/browser-playwright": "^4.0.18",
		"eslint": "^9.39.2",
		"eslint-config-prettier": "^10.1.8",
		"eslint-plugin-svelte": "^3.14.0",
		"fast-check": "^4.5.3",
		"globals": "^17.3.0",
		"mdsvex": "^0.12.6",
		"playwright": "^1.58.2",
		"prettier": "^3.8.1",
		"prettier-plugin-svelte": "^3.4.1",
		"svelte": "^5.51.0",
		"svelte-check": "^4.4.2",
		"typescript": "^5.9.3",
		"typescript-eslint": "^8.54.0",
		"vite": "^7.3.1",
		"vitest": "^4.0.18",
		"vitest-browser-svelte": "^2.0.2"
	}
}

lines: -    "prepare": "svelte-kit sync || echo '' && git config core.hooksPath hooks",
+    "prepare": "svelte-kit sync || true && (test -d .git && git config core.hooksPath hooks || true)",

input.description: Verify prepare script still works locally

content.content:
> claudia@0.0.1 prepare
> svelte-kit sync || true && (test -d .git && git config core.hooksPath hooks || true)

toolUseResult.stdout:
> claudia@0.0.1 prepare
> svelte-kit sync || true && (test -d .git && git config core.hooksPath hooks || true)

input.command:
git add package.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Guard git config in prepare script against CI environments

npm ci runs the prepare hook; in CI there's no .git dir so
`git config core.hooksPath hooks` was failing. Guard with
`test -d .git` so it only runs in a real checkout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
